Minimum_Safe_Calorie_Floor.Rule: Final_kcal must not drop below RER * 1.0 unless medically overridden

BCS_Target.note: No formal BCS; ensure steady gain and weight doubling by day 10

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Long bones mainly cartilaginous; secondary ossification not visible

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: High vulnerability to growth failure if underfed or hypothermic

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Colostrum and maternal milk only; passive immunity establishment

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: No forced handling; gentle positioning only

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Mineralization starts in proximal femoral and tibial epiphyses

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Avoid slips and falls on slick flooring

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: RER-based maternal intake; early weaning slurry by 3-4 weeks

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Short, supervised exploration on non-slippery surfaces

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Deciduous dentition erupts; passive Ca absorption window opens

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Excess calcium or calories can overload immature joints

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Transition to solid puppy food; MER 3x RER

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Low-impact play; no stairs or jumping off furniture

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Rapid limb elongation; all secondary ossification centers visible

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Overfeeding accelerates DOD risk

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: MER begins shifting from 3x to 2x RER by 16 weeks

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Controlled play; avoid forced running or repetitive fetch

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Peak growth velocity; rapid diaphyseal expansion

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Highest DOD and hip/elbow dysplasia expression if overweight

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Energy-intensive tissue accretion; strict Ca:P control

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Short walks and play; avoid jumping off heights

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Anconeal process of ulna should fuse by 5 months

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Risk of UAP and OCD if overfed or over-exercised

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: MER stabilizing toward 2x RER

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Gentle leash walks; no forced jogging or agility

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Distal limb growth plates narrowing

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Early estrus in females; joint cartilage still immature

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Growth rate slowing; watch for creeping adiposity

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Increase walk duration; still avoid high impact

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Distal humerus and proximal ulna physes closing

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: High risk if overweight and over-exercised

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Sex hormones shaping bone density and musculature

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Moderate daily exercise; swimming ideal

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Stature (height) largely stabilized

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Hip and elbow screening window opens

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Transition from growth to maintenance density diets

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Controlled off-leash play; avoid repetitive jumping

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Most major long-bone physes closed by 12 months

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Early OA signs if previously overloaded

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: MER transitions to adult factors x RER

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Build endurance; still limit intense impact sports

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Tibial tuberosity and proximal tibia plates in final fusion

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Monitor for residual DOD, lameness

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: First stable adult weight; MER individualized by activity

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Progressive conditioning; introduce low-level sport work

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Final skeletal consolidation; joint loading patterns set

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Manage weight to prevent early OA

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Metabolic plateau; energy tied to workload

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Full adult exercise with gradual intensity increases

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: All physes closed; subchondral bone density still adapting

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Cumulative joint load becomes key

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Adult maintenance; adjust for sport vs pet lifestyle

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Can safely perform most athletic tasks with conditioning

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: OFA hip/elbow certification age; final adult conformation

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Onset of chronic OA can be delayed by lean BCS

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Peak athletic capacity

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: High-intensity work allowed with proper warm-up

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Stable structure; focus on maintaining LBM

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Weight gain accelerates OA risk

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Highest metabolic efficiency and work capacity

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Daily vigorous exercise; sport or working tasks ideal

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Microdamage accumulates if overweight or overworked

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: High risk of creeping obesity with reduced activity

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: MER may start to decline if activity drops

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Maintain regular, joint-friendly exercise

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Early degenerative changes possible in hips/elbows

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: OA risk rises if BCS > 5

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: MER may decrease 0.10 by end of this phase

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Regular low-impact exercise; avoid sudden high-intensity bursts

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: OA and spinal changes more common

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Marked OA risk if overweight

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Metabolic slowdown 0.15; sarcopenia begins

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Daily gentle walks and swimming; avoid repetitive impact

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: High prevalence of OA; spinal and joint stiffness

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: Body weight becomes major survival hazard factor

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Fat mass tends to increase; lean survivors maintain better LBM

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Short, frequent, low-impact sessions; hydrotherapy ideal

BCS_Target.note: with priority on muscle over fat

Skeletal_Maturity_Note.content: Significant sarcopenia and OA common

Orthopedic_Risk_Window.content: High risk of mobility loss and chronic pain

Metabolic_Shift_Note.content: Lower calorie needs but higher protein density required

Exercise_Safety_Recommendation.content: Therapeutic, assisted exercise; focus on comfort and mobility

Indian_Home_Foods: Rice with milk (100g; skip milk if lactose sensitive)

descriptive_note.content: Dam’s milk; high fat, high protein

data.Commercial_Option: Not recommended; dam or veterinary milk replacer
data.Precautions: Maintain warmth and ensure weight gain

descriptive_note.content: High protein, moderate fat, controlled Ca:P

data.Weekly_Meal_Flow: Gradual shift from milk to soft mash by 8 weeks
data.Supplements: Avoid Ca supplements; balanced puppy diet only
data.Precautions: Prevent rapid weight spikes; monitor stool

descriptive_note.content: High protein, adequate fat, controlled minerals

data.Supplements: Joint or omega-3 only if vet recommends
data.Precautions: Keep BCS lean; avoid overfeeding for 'chubby puppy' look

descriptive_note.content: Growth: protein-dense, moderate fat, strict Ca:P

data.Weekly_Meal_Flow: Stable 3 meals with gradual portion increases
data.Precautions: Peak growth; strict weight and BCS monitoring

data.Weekly_Meal_Flow: Consistent meals; energy adjusted by BCS
data.Supplements: Joint support optional if high activity
data.Precautions: Distal limb physes closing; avoid impact sports

descriptive_note.content: Transition from growth to adult; still growth-focused

data.Weekly_Meal_Flow: Gradual shift to adult maintenance by 10-12 months
data.Precautions: Do not switch to low-protein senior diets

Weekly_Meal_Plan_Examples: Khichdi + boiled chicken + veggies (2 main meals)

Senior_Stage_Nutrition_7Y_plus.Kidney_Care_Notes: Maintain good hydration, avoid excess phosphorus and very high sodium, monitor creatinine/SDMA with vet
Senior_Stage_Nutrition_7Y_plus.Sarcopenia_Risk_Note: Monitor Muscle Condition Score; stable weight can hide muscle loss
Easy_Chew_Food_Suggestions: Soaked kibble or soft commercial senior diets

Recommended_Foods: Balanced khichdi with chicken and veggies
Boiled chicken with rice and carrots
Avoid_Foods: Large, heavy meals right before intense exercise

descriptive_note.content: Higher protein and moderate fat; maintain balanced Ca:P

Underweight.Diet_Modification_Guidance: Add egg mash, chicken stew, sweet potato, increase portion size gradually

descriptive_note.content: Maintain current balance; prioritize lean proteins and moderate fat

Ideal.Diet_Modification_Guidance: Keep BCS at 4 to 5; adjust ±0.05 to 0.10 calories based on seasonal activity

descriptive_note.content: Lower fat, higher fiber, adequate protein

Overweight.Diet_Modification_Guidance: Use pumpkin rice, boiled veggies, curd; avoid high-fat treats

descriptive_note.content: High protein to protect LBM; low fat; high fiber

Obese.Diet_Modification_Guidance: Clinical weight-loss plan; measured portions, vet-supervised, target gradual loss

Fever.When_To_See_Vet: If temperature >103°F, refuses food/water, or fever >24h

Loose_Motion.When_To_See_Vet: If blood/mucus present, severe lethargy, or >24h duration

Vomiting.When_To_See_Vet: Immediate vet if repeated vomiting, blood, or associated with collapse

Lethargy.When_To_See_Vet: If lethargy >72h or associated with other red-flag signs

Recommended_Foods: Novel protein as advised by vet
Skin_Allergy.When_To_See_Vet: If intense itching, hair loss, or skin wounds are present

Joint_Pain.When_To_See_Vet: At first persistent lameness or reluctance to move/jump

Summer.Food_Modification_Guidance: Use more curd rice, lauki mash, light meals; avoid very heavy/fatty meals

Winter.Food_Modification_Guidance: Use warm khichdi, chicken stew, sweet potato; slightly higher fat allowed

Rainy.Rainy_Hygiene_Rule: Keep bowls dry and clean; avoid stale wet food
Rainy.Food_Modification_Guidance: Prefer easily digestible foods like moong dal khichdi, pumpkin puree, curd rice

Hydration_System.Override_Selection_Rule: If multiple hydration triggers apply, select highest multiplier only

Prohibited_Foods_List.Toxic_Reason: Contains theobromine and caffeine causing cardiac, neurologic and GI toxicity
Can cause acute kidney failure in dogs
Cause oxidative damage to red blood cells leading to anemia
Triggers severe hypoglycemia and possible liver failure
Depresses CNS and can cause metabolic acidosis
Splintering risk; can cause GI obstruction or perforation
Increase risk of pancreatitis and obesity

Longevity_Obesity_Research_Data.Obesity_Prevalence_Note: Overweight condition in mid-life strongly associated with higher OA and earlier mortality
Longevity_Obesity_Research_Data.BCS_Greater_Than_5_Risk_Note: BCS > 5 associated with higher OA prevalence and reduced healthy lifespan

Large_Breed_Calcium_Safety_Rule.Warning_Against_Supplementation: Avoid additional calcium supplements when feeding a complete large-breed puppy diet; excess Ca during 0-6 months can cause DOD/OCD

Ideal_Mid_Weight_Selection.Selection_Rule: Find age bracket (with interpolation if needed), compute mid for given sex: Mid = (Min + Max) / 2

Questions.Prompt: Can you easily feel ribs with light pressure without deep pressing?
Is a clear waist visible from above?
Is there an abdominal tuck when viewed from the side?

Fusion_Model.Conflict_Resolution_Rule: If rounded category differs from either component category, choose higher risk category
Fusion_Model.Edge_Case_Rule: If one component returns range (e.g., 8-9), use midpoint before weighting

Age_Resolution_Control.Matching_Rule: Match smallest valid numeric age window first
Age_Resolution_Control.Unit_Normalization: Convert weeks and years into months before comparison
Age_Resolution_Control.Conflict_Handling: If overlapping, choose most specific (narrowest range)

Stage_Resolver_Engine.Matching_Rule: Match numeric min/max boundaries; prioritize most specific age bracket

Base_Calorie_Derivation.Step_2_Select_MER: MER selected from Energy_System.MER_Multipliers based on lifecycle stage and neuter/activity status
Base_Calorie_Derivation.Note: All subsequent adjustments are additive percentages applied to Base

MER_Application_Rule.Selection_Basis: Lifecycle stage + neuter status + baseline activity

Master_Calorie_Adjustment_Pipeline.Formula: max(RER * 1.0, Base * (1 + clamp(Activity_Adjustment + Weight_Adjustment + Symptom_Adjustment + Seasonal_Adjustment + Senior_Adjustment, -0.35, 0.35)))
Master_Calorie_Adjustment_Pipeline.Senior_Adjustment_Rule: If age ≥ 7 years, apply stage-defined slowdown percent as negative adjustment

Global_Adjustment_Cap.Rule: Clamp final additive adjustment before multiplication

Master_Calorie_Adjustment_Pipeline.Seasonal_Conflict_Rule: If both seasonal systems exist, ignore Diet_Plan_Seasonal for calculations
Master_Calorie_Adjustment_Pipeline.Conflict_Rule: If multiple negative adjustments, choose most restrictive unless medical override present
Master_Calorie_Adjustment_Pipeline.Hydration_Integration: Apply Hydration_System overrides after final calorie resolution

Global_Adjustment_Control.Safety_Rule: If cumulative adjustment exceeds cap, clamp to cap value

Seasonal_Calorie_Control.Conflict_Rule: Apply only one seasonal calorie modifier at a time

Test_Cases.Validation_Target: Final calories should reflect +activity and +winter shift only
Hydration override applied; calorie reduction applied once

Hydration_Stack_Control.Rule: Apply only the highest single multiplier

If_BCS_Obese_or_Overweight.Transition_Rule: Switch to Lean_Muscle_Optimization when BCS reaches 4-5

Weight_Loss_Goal.Override_Rule: Always respect medical symptom restrictions first

Allowed.name: Surmai / Seer
Allowed.note: deboned, boiled — limited due to mercury risk

Animal_Protein_Control.Enforcement_Rule: Reject recipe if any protein outside Allowed list is present

regulatory_reference.source_alignment: AAFCO Official Publication 2023, NRC 2006 Canine Nutrient Requirements
regulatory_reference.use_case: Corrective calcium supplementation for homemade diet balancing

Vegetable_Safety_List.description: Used to prevent fiber overload, gas risk and inappropriate lifecycle usage

Weekly_Meal_Cycle_System.Description: Deterministic 7-day rotation — grams calculated from final kcal target

Action_Rules.low: Simplify next cycle: reduce number of different ingredients, keep same calorie target
Action_Rules.partial: Keep current complexity, remind accurate weighing
Action_Rules.good: Maintain or slightly increase variety if user requests

Weight_Change_Rules.if_exceeds: Adjust final_kcal ±5% toward ideal direction (respect Global_Adjustment_Cap)

Compliance_Adaptive_Control.Rollover_Trigger: After day 7 → request new weight → recalculate BCS → regenerate cycle

Scientific_Reference_Sources.AAFCO_Version: AAFCO 2023 Official Publication - Dog Nutrient Profiles for Growth and Reproduction (All Life Stages except Lactation) and Adult Maintenance
Scientific_Reference_Sources.NRC_Reference: National Research Council (2006) Nutrient Requirements of Dogs and Cats, National Academies Press
Scientific_Reference_Sources.Alignment_Statement_1: All lifecycle minimums (protein, fat) align with AAFCO official large-breed growth and maintenance standards
Scientific_Reference_Sources.Alignment_Statement_2: Calcium safety values are NRC-aligned for large-breed puppies (3.0–4.5 g Ca per 1000 kcal ME during growth phases; Ca:P ratio 1.1:1 to 1.4:1)

Formulas.Residual_Reconciliation: If total_energy_fraction != 1.0, adjust Carbohydrate_g by difference (±2% tolerance allowed)
Formulas.Rounding_Rule: Round to nearest integer gram only after all allocations; no intermediate rounding

Macro_Conversion_Engine.Selected_Macro_Profile_Source: Macronutrient_Ratio_Profiles based on Goal_Override_Controller or default Maintenance

Validation_Rules.During_Growth: Reject if Calcium_per_1000_kcal not in [3.0, 4.5] or Ca_to_P_ratio not in [1.1, 1.4]

Mineral_Computation_Engine.Reference: Scientific_Reference_Sources.NRC_Reference (Calcium and Phosphorus Requirements for Large-Breed Growth)

Validation_Rules.Lifecycle_Detection: Use Lifecycle_Growth_Model_20_Stages to determine Growth or Adult

fields.macro_profile: object {protein: number, fat: number, carbs: number, fiber: number}
fields.meal_cycle: array of daily plans {day: integer, meals: array of {kcal: number, ingredients: array of {name: string, grams: number}}}

Rules.If_Compliance_Greater_Than_0.85: Allow deterministic rotation from Ingredient_Substitution_Map (no randomness)

Compliance_Based_Simplification_Logic.Rotation_Rule: Cycle through substitution lists deterministically (e.g., index = week_number % list_length)

Absolute_Calorie_Safety_Limits.Reference: Scientific_Reference_Sources.NRC_Reference (Safe Energy Intake Ranges to Prevent Under/Overfeeding)

Plateau_Detection_Logic.Detection_Rule: If abs(Weight_Change_Pct) < 0.005 for 2 consecutive weeks, plateau detected

Adjustment_Rule.If_Plateau: Adjust Final_kcal_per_day by ±0.05 (positive if goal is gain/underweight, negative if loss/overweight)

Plateau_Detection_Logic.Reference: Scientific_Reference_Sources.AAFCO_Version (Guidelines for Adaptive Feeding in Weight Management)

Ratio_Protection_Layer.Recalculate_After_Add: Total_Calcium_g = Current_Total_Calcium_g + (Supplement_grams_required * Selected_Source.calcium_g_per_gram); Total_Phosphorus_g = Current_Total_Phosphorus_g + (Supplement_grams_required * Selected_Source.phosphorus_g_per_gram); Ca_to_P_ratio = Total_Calcium_g / Total_Phosphorus_g
Ratio_Protection_Layer.Adjustment_Rule: If Ca_to_P_ratio > 1.4, reduce Supplement_grams_required by 0.05 decrements until Ca_to_P_ratio <= 1.4 or Supplement_grams_required = 0; If Ca_to_P_ratio < 1.1, reject and flag for manual vet adjustment (no auto-increase P)

Calcium_Supplementation_Module.Overdose_Protection: Reject if Calcium_per_1000_kcal > Nutrient_Standards.Growth_Large_Breed.Calcium_g_per_1000_kcal_Range[1] or Supplement_grams_required > Selected_Source.max_safe_gram_per_kg_bodyweight * weight_kg

Allowed.name: Surmai / Seer
Allowed.note: deboned, boiled — limited due to mercury risk

Animal_Protein_Control.Enforcement_Rule: Reject recipe if any protein outside Allowed list is present or organ exceeds cap

Senior_Phosphorus_Control_Module.Controlled_Protein_Source_Substitution_L: Replace high-P protein (e.g., Chicken liver boiled) with low-P alternative from Food_Composition_Database (e.g., Cod fish boiled deboned); Recalculate Total_Phosphorus_g; Repeat until Phosphorus_per_1000_kcal <= 0.5 or no low-P options left (then reject)
Senior_Phosphorus_Control_Module.Deterministic_Phosphorus_Reduction_Rule: Reduce portion of highest P-density ingredient by 0.1 decrements until compliant or min portion reached

User_Complexity_Adaptive_Module_v2.Deterministic_Rotation: Use Ingredient_Substitution_Map with fixed order based on week_number % map_length
User_Complexity_Adaptive_Module_v2.Optional_Toggle: User can select 'Basic Mode' (max 3 ingredients, no rotation) or 'Advanced Mode' (full rotation allowed if compliance >0.85)
